title: Properties and Behavior of a Regular Stochastic Matrix
description: A visual explanation of how a regular stochastic matrix ensures convergence to a unique steady-state vector regardless of the initial state.
elements: Convergence Paths from Different Initial States
relationships: v0 is multiplied by P to get a new state vector
Repeated multiplication by P (applying P^k) transforms any v0
As k → ∞, P^k converges to a matrix with identical rows
All rows of the limiting matrix are the steady-state vector π
All convergence paths lead to the same π, proving uniqueness
